summary: This table is stretched: Korlash is trying to play a slower battlecruiser game while Tymna and Thrasios can pressure the game with compact combo lines, and Atraxa sits between them with resilient planeswalker value.

bracket_spread.headline: Mixed pod from Core to cEDH
bracket_spread.verdict: The pod is mismatched unless the cEDH deck self-handicaps or swaps out.
bracket_spread.rationale: The highest deck can threaten protected wins well before the slowest deck is meaningfully online, so the table is likely to revolve around stopping the cEDH seat.
evidence: Korlash is a Bracket 2 mono-black precon-style deck
Tymna and Thrasios shows Thassa's Oracle plus consultation-style pressure
Atraxa has planeswalker value and a stronger midgame than Korlash

rule_zero_brief.headline: Agree on whether this is a high-power game before shuffling.
rule_zero_brief.pregame_template: This pod spans B2 to B5. Tymna/Thrasios has compact combo pressure; Korlash is much slower. Are we playing the cEDH deck straight, asking it to slow down, or swapping decks?

talking_points.prompt: Can the Tymna/Thrasios pilot explain whether early two-card wins are live this game?
Does everyone want a high-power game, or should the strongest deck change?

disclosures.label: Compact combo
disclosures.detail: One deck can end the game much earlier than the others are built to answer.

power.rationale: The pod's ceiling is set by the cEDH seat rather than the average deck.

speed.rationale: One deck can pressure the game before the lower bracket deck develops.

interaction.rationale: The strongest deck likely has efficient protection, but the table as a whole is uneven.

consistency.rationale: The high-end deck is consistent enough to define the game, while the lower decks are less reliable.

salt.rationale: A fast mismatch can create frustration even when the cards themselves are legal.

social_friction.rationale: This table needs an explicit Rule 0 agreement before play.

decks.rationale: This seat is likely behind on speed and stack interaction.
Atraxa can dominate longer games but is still slower than the combo seat.
This seat sets the table's real speed and interaction requirements.

friction_drivers.label: Bracket mismatch
friction_drivers.explanation: The lowest and highest seats are separated by three brackets.

recommendations.title: Swap or self-handicap the cEDH seat
recommendations.detail: The cleanest fair game is to replace Tymna/Thrasios with a Bracket 3 deck, or have that pilot disclose and avoid early combo lines.
